message:
fix(compat): use asupersync async Mutex in Tower bridge; remove debug logging from lab scheduler

Tower bridge: replaces std::sync::Mutex with asupersync::sync::Mutex for
the inner service, enabling proper async locking during poll_ready. The
previous std Mutex could not be held across await points, and the
lock().expect() pattern would panic on poisoning. The async Mutex
provides cancel-aware locking via lock(cx).await and into_inner()
without the poisoning concern. The lock is now held across the entire
poll_ready→call sequence and explicitly dropped before awaiting the
response future.

lab/runtime: removes leftover DEBUG println statements from
LabScheduler::is_empty that were added during debugging and should not
have been committed. The is_empty method returns self.scheduled.is_empty()
directly again.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
